text: I 100% recommend taking this kayaking tour whether you are a tourist or a Las Vegas native. Calvin (head guide) and Emma (in training) both did a great job and made sure we got in/out of our kayaks safely and had everything we needed on the tour. We had a lot of fun learning neat facts while paddling down the river while getting a refreshing splash when a water gun fight would break out. There is a wait when you finally get to Emerald Cave due to its popularity, but they took that time to pass out snacks and additional water. Definitely don't forget the sunscreen! The kayaks are really nice and actually have a rudder and pedals to help steer which I wasn't used to. I would definitely do it again.

Emerald cave and the hoover damn were some of the highlights of our vegas trip. We got to check off Nevada and Arizona from states we visited this year. This was our first time kayaking and Jeff was extremely knowledgeable, kind, and a great photographer. I would highly recommend booking with evolution expeditions. Jeff also had waters, snacks, and lunch for us. He made for an inclusive and wonderful experience.

Thank you so much for the kind words! We’re really glad you had such a fun day out on the water with us. Faith will be happy to hear she made the experience memorable—she really loves what she does. It’s great to know you enjoyed the mix of activities (and the snacks help too!). We appreciate the recommendation and hope to see you again on another adventure soon.- Evolution Expeditions Kayaking Las Vegas

Kayaking down the Colorado River to Emerald Cave was exhilarating. My family and I enjoyed the scenery and beautiful crystal clear water. Super Easy 2 mile trek to and from the Cave.  Although the expectations of the Cave were on a bigger scale it still was nice to see amazing crystal clear emerald colored water. Thanks to our awesome guide Emma who made this tour easy, fun, adventurous and informative. Would definitely recommend!!

Thank you for sharing your feedback and for joining Evolution Expeditions.You’re absolutely right that wait times to enter the cave can vary, especially during busy periods, and we appreciate you calling that out for future guests. Conditions like crowd levels and timing on the water are factors we don’t always control, but we do our best to set expectations and guide our groups to make the most of the experience.Even when lines are longer, our goal is to ensure guests still enjoy a scenic, memorable paddle with plenty to see along the way. We also encourage early departures whenever possible to help maximize time at key spots like the cave.

We enjoyed the activity and the tour guide Emma was lovely, however the transport arrangements were a little unorganised causing us to arrive back 2 hours late which mucked up other plans. It would also be helpful if the ad mentioned an option to swim and suggest clothing the wear. We turned up not dressed appropriately and would loved to have taken a swim.

My wife and I were very pleased with our kayaking experience delivered by Evolution Expeditions.  The upfront coordination and communication by Maggie was solid and clear.  Our trip guide, Soren, was outstanding!  He was very helpful and thorough in handling all of the details for the trip.  He added a good balance of providing insight & background on the area and its sights.In conclusion, we strongly endorse Evolution Expeditions as a provider of experiences.  Finally we highly recommend Soren as a guide.👍

Some of us had experience, some had none, so this is something that can be done by varying degrees of skill. The lunch provided was a great addition! This trip was my favorite part of Vegas, would highly recommend!

Thank you for the fantastic review. Our tour guy was excellent! We truly had an amazing and relaxing time.  We were so worried at first about it being too cold this time of year, but we had perfect weather and we didn’t get wet or even damp. The actual cave was not what we thought - a small nook. However, the entire adventure on getting there was amazing. Our kids had a blast. The tandem kayaks were perfect! Easy to use.I should mention, the tour was in Nevada time so make sure you clarify this.  Water shoes are a must. Also, don’t be late!! You hold up the tour for everyone else.

Faith was a wonderful tour guide! Her knowledge of the river, the history, the animals, and the plant life was impressive! We learned so much! She was receptive to everyone's needs and abilities. We highly recommend this tour!

We had a wonderful day enjoying paddling up the Colorado River to see the unique green shimmering affect of thr Emerald Cave. Jayden was great guide and Kristen was super responsive by text making the arrangements. I would highly recommend this experience!
Great scenic trip w some history . Kayaks were in good shape.great fun and guide was knowledgeable would highly recommend

He told us all a detailed history of the region and was very knowledgeable about the flora and fauna.  We also got some tips on what to do in Vegas the next day.  10/10 so glad I booked this tour!

site:Our kayaking trip to the Emerald Cave on the Colorado River was absolutely fantastic! Soren, our guide, was incredibly kind and attentive — he made sure no kayaker was ever left behind and kept the group together the whole way. He shared so much interesting information about the history, nature, and environment of the area, which made the experience even more meaningful.The weather couldn’t have been more perfect, and the clear green water truly lived up to its “Emerald” name — it was stunning. All the equipment was in great condition, the instructions were clear and easy to follow, and everything ran smoothly and right on schedule.It was the perfect mix of adventure, relaxation, and learning. Highly recommend this trip to anyone looking for a memorable day on the Colorado River!

We’re thrilled you had such an amazing adventure! 🐏 Spotting a flock of bighorn sheep is always a special treat, and we’re glad our guides and top-notch gear helped make the day smooth and fun. With gorgeous scenery all around, it sounds like a truly memorable paddle. Can’t wait to welcome you back for your next great adventure!-Evolution Expedition Kayaking, Las Vegas

We’re so glad you and your husband had a blast in the tandem kayak! 🚣‍♀️ Ray loves keeping things fun, easy, and full of laughs, and it sounds like the perfect match for a day on those stunning green waters. Clear skies, calm paddling, and great company—what more could you ask for? Can’t wait to see you back on the water!-Evolution Expedition Kayaking, Las Vegas

A group of girlfriends came to visit and we booked this tour for a Saturday. Faith was our guide and we could not have had a better time! The kayaks were well maintained and stable. Faith was so knowledgeable and prepared to share interesting information while on the river regarding the history and the environment. Emerald Cove was gorgeous and we had a lot of photo ops! Definitely one of my favorite ways to enjoy the Colorado River.

We’re so happy you enjoyed the crystal-clear waters and stunning views! Our guides love sharing interesting stories to make every paddle both relaxing and memorable 🌊🏞️😄. Thanks for the high recommendation!-Evolution Expeditions Kayaking, Las Vegas

Went on the 3 hour Express Emerald Cave Tour with Faith and it was one of the best days trips we’ve ever been on! Faith was a big part of us having such a positive experience. She was helpful and informative and really just made it a great time for everyone!! The cave was beautiful and the short stop looking at the historical site fun. I would highly recommend and will likely do it again next year!

We’re thrilled you had such an amazing adventure! 🚣‍♂️ Emerald Cave has a way of impressing everyone, no matter your kayaking experience, and our crew loves showing it off. Can’t wait to welcome you back for another must-do day on the water!-Evolution Expedition Kayaking, Las Vegas

Ever find yourself in Vegas and wish you could take a break from the insanity? This tour offers breathtaking views and ice cold emerald green water on the Colorado River below Hoover Dam.  No paddling experience required! Our guide, Faith was top notch and I don’t know where you’re gonna find this much fun for such a reasonable price in Vegas. They make it super easy and pick you up from your hotel too! If you’re a nature person stuck in Vegas for work and have an afternoon off, Evolution kayak tours are a no brainer!

Welcome back for round two on the river — we love repeat paddlers! 🛶 Those easy-to-steer rudder kayaks really let you soak in the stunning scenery, crystal-clear water, and all the wildlife surprises along the way (bighorn sheep sightings are always a highlight!). Soren will be thrilled to hear your kind words — his knowledge, organization, and focus on safety help make every trip smooth and unforgettable. Thanks for choosing to adventure with us again, and we hope to see you out on the water for round three soon!-Evolution Expedition Kayaking, Las Vegas

The lake is beautiful, all levels of skill can participate (my husband has some limitations and was worried but it was all good) and the pace is just right.Pointers: sunscreen/hats for the bright sun and  the  road from the freeway exit  to the beach is not well identified  (we did not see any signs )but it's the only way to the beach. Once at the beach, it's full of hustle/bustle of water sport enthusiasts!

Beautiful paddle on the Colorado River. Our guide was friendly and knowledgeable. The only part we hoped we had more of the cave part of the paddle. That part is short.

Our guide Maddy was super! Excellent communication, very friendly, informative and engaged with all members of tour. Tour itself was also great although we waited in line for 45 minutes to enter the Emerald cave, which was out of their control. Maddy even went above and beyond taking us to a park to view longhorn sheep! Only downside is we had no food other than a small snack for a tour beginning with pick up at 9:10 and dropped back off at 4:30.

Jeff, was a great, interesting and well informed guide. Engaged well with all making the time go too quickly.Only minor issue was starting late. We were fine on time so no big deal, but getting there the requested 30 minutes early and leaving the dock nearly 30 minutes late hopefully not the norm. Only reason A 4 vs a 5.

As a group of 3 first-time kayakers, this was an once-in-a-lifetime experience. Kayaking down between the Black Canyon is absolutely breathtaking. From beginning to end, every single one of the Evolution Expeditions staff was professional, courteous and knowledgeable. All staff communicated very clearly and you could sense that safety was a top priority, which was reassuring. Soren, our guide, was excellent. Not only was he a well-equipped leader, he added value to our journey by giving us insight and history of Black Canyon, Emerald Cave and Hoover Dam. The river was busy with various tour companies and kayakers, but our group of 6 kayaks never felt abandoned or lost. Soren facilitated our cave entry by trying a group maneuver which saved time and energy, which was fantastic to see in action and greatly appreciated. On our way back to the beach, we were paddling against the wind which was challenging, but not impossible and we made it back to the beach safe and sound. Soren was well prepared with snacks and water for us which was refreshing and also appreciated. I cannot recommend Evolution Expeditions and our guide, Soren enough. Do not think twice and book!

This was a GREAT experience kayaking on the Colorado River.  Jeff was an awesome guide.  His love for and knowledge of the area was evident.  I loved hearing all of the history of that section of the river.  He's a wealth of information!  Of everything we have done while in the Las Vegas area, this was one of the best.  Our group of four friends (age 65 to 71)  did the 4 hr kayak trip.  The lunch from Jason's Deli was good and fresh.  The temps were high (108 or so), but it didn't feel that hot because the water was a nice 53 degrees.  So, don't let the temps concern you.  We were able to get in the water a couple of times (on the shore---don't worry, you won't have to get back on the kayak while in deep water).  Definitely do this tour while you're in the area.  A little less than 1 hr drive from LV....the drive was nice with lots of mountains to look at.  This would be good for kids.  Tandem kayaks are available and a parent could definitely handle the kayak with a small child in the front.  Thanks, Jeff for a great experience!  Thanks for being so easy to talk with.

We had a great time kayaking on the Colorado River with our tour guide, Maggie.  I initially wasn’t sure how difficult this would be but it was not hard at all.  We luckily saw quite a few BigHorn Sheep grazing and multiple species of birds and fish.  Maggie was very knowledgeable on the animals and native plants in the area.  Although the temp was over 100 on the day we went, it was not noticeable as the water temp was 53.  We went on the 9am tour which meant we did not get to see the beautiful green water in the cave because the area was shaded but we did not have to wait to go in the cave which was nice.  Maggie did mention that tours in the afternoon have to wait sometimes over an hour to go in due to more people taking tours at that time when the green water is most vibrant.  We still were able to see the green water across from the cave and it was beautiful.  I can’t comment on the transportation because we drove ourselves.  I highly recommend this tour.

Great day out - thoroughly recommend. We were running late (due to issues at car hire at airport) but we messaged ahead and they were very accommodating. There were kayaks in our tour of different abilities. Calvin had great knowledge and was very friendly- the snacks and cold drinks were a great addition. Despite being out in the sun for 3 hrs we did not feel too hot ( although my technique at using the oars ensured I was soaking wet). Good fun with the water guns which were also provided.

We really enjoyed our tour especially with our guide Jeff. He is really passionate with his job and even more with Mother Nature. We really appreciated all the explanations about Las Vegas and the Hoover Dam. Going to the Emerald Cave was really nice. We had a beautiful day. The Tour was great thanks to our guide. Kayaking was easy and it was beautiful to see the Mountains.

The kayaking tour was a thoroughly enjoyable experience, and it exceeded expectations. The scenery was beautiful, and the paddling was great.The tour was made even better by the guide, Petra. Petra was helpful, fun, and knowledgeable about the area. She answered questions and provided insights throughout the excursion. The group felt safe and supported during the trip.Petra's professionalism and engaging personality made the kayaking adventure special. I would definitely recommend this excursion.

Thank you so much for the fantastic review! We’re thrilled to hear you had an amazing first kayaking experience with Evolution Expeditions. We appreciate you choosing Evolution Expeditions and can’t wait to welcome you back for another adventure!

My brother and I had a great time kayaking down the river and back. The scenery was amazing, and the water was relatively calm and easy to navigate as a novice kayaker. The two tour guides Jeff and Ray were great fun whilst out on the river and were very informative about the scenery and local history of the area.This is a trip I'd highly recommend for the experience, and it only takes up half a day compared with a full day when heading to the Grand Canyon.

My husband and I decided to take this tour while visiting my daughter who was in Las Vegas for an internship, looking for something "outdoorsy" that we could do in the heat of summer!  The tour was wonderful!  Our guide was Jeffrey, and he was extremely friendly and knowledgable.  He drove the shuttle van from the strip and told us all sorts of interesting things about Vegas, the Hoover Dam, and Boulder City along the way.  The kayaking trip itself was beautiful, and with the cold (frigid!) water it was easy to stay comfortable in the heat.  As others have mentioned, if you're hoping to explore deep inside a big cave, you'll be disappointed...the cave itself is just sort of a nook in the side of the rocks that we call put our kayaks in for a photo up.  But the river and canyon itself is gorgeous, as is the emerald water.  We had easy paddling on the way out but it was a bit more challenging on the way back due to headwinds, but for us it was a welcome bit of exercise...just something to consider for those looking for pure leisure.  Jeffrey took us for a side stop in Boulder City to see the bighorn sheep at a city park...a unique and fun addition to the day.

Little sloppy getting started but that was overshadowed by the amazing guide, Cameron who took our group out and was fantastic. We paddled to the Emerald Cave ( Cameron's call) versus stopping along the way and we got to the cave before a few other groups making their way to the cave, great call which saved us time not having to wait to enter the cave. Scenery along the paddle is spectacular, water temperature is cooler which helped on a 100 degree day. Would recommend this tour group.

Thank you for sharing your experience! We’re so glad you enjoyed the stunning water, refreshing temps, and rich history of the area. Jeff and Calvin will be delighted to know they helped make your trip amazing. We truly appreciate you choosing Evolution Expeditions and hope to see you on the river again soon!

Not only was the cove beautiful, the whole experience was great. Our guide Calvin was awesome. He was fun, knowledgeable and took the time to get to know all of us in the group. The journey to the cove was about 2 miles. My husband and I were each in our own kayak. If you’ve not been kayaking before, it may be a bit challenging to keep up, especially on the 2 mile journey back to the beach. It’s very hot but the water temp stays cold at around 53 degrees, which felt amazing and helps to stay cool. Overall experience was great. Would definitely recommend.

We did the hotel pickup and it was very easy and comfortable.  The driver was our tour guide.  She offered information along the way and provided water bottles.  The tour was terrific.  It was great to escape the hot Las Vegas strip and enjoy nature.   I would highly recommend this tour!

Jeff was our tour guide, and he was phenomenal! Really funny and overall a good guy (also used to work as a DEA agent before retiring to do this!)  The hardest part was honestly the 1 hour of rowing in the kayak, as our hands were sore after, but overall it was worth it for the views of the valley and the overall great time we had learning about the area. We even had a chance to stop at a Hemenway Park on the way back to see Bighorn sheep walking around close to people, and a look at Lake Mead.

I’m so happy my family decided to take this excursion while visiting Vegas. We did the self-drive option taking us through the desert and was able to stop at the Hover Dam on the way back. The water was incredibly refreshing and perfect for the heat. Our tour guide was amazing and well equipped to lead us. I’d suggest this to anyone who likes nature or wants to get away from the fast-paced  strip for a little bit. Tips: Stay hydrated and be prepared to get in knee deep water to board and land. Wear a hat or shades. There are water safe spots to store clothes or bags. Even if you can’t swim, the kayaks are stable and a life jacket is required, so don’t be afraid! Lastly, take lots of photos and enjoy!

But going down stream was hard because we were going against the wind. You really had your push. If you’re not that strong in the arm rowing, then go in a doubles kayak to share the rowing load. For small kids, you want to double up. Going 4 miles is no joke. Oh, make sure to properly put on the life vest because one person tipped over.

Rented a tandem kayak and was pleasantly surprised it was the sit in type with a rudder. Very nicely paced with lots of history and nature information from our amazing guide Burton. There was ample parking near restrooms near the boat launch. We were fitted for the rudder pedals and our life jackets 20mins before launch time and took off on time. The expedition tour company provided water, juice, and snacks. We kayaked down about 2miles to emerald cave. Along the way, we stopped to see local vegetation and saw how scientists measured the fault lines to determine where Hoover dam would be. Halfway, we were able to take a quick dip in the water. There was a short wait to let another tour company out of the emerald cave and then our entire group of kayaks went in to view the cave. On the way back, we saw turtles, ducks, and finally a few sheep. Very worth it. I’d want to go again.

What a fantastic and memorable day on the Colorado River! Jeff was knowledgeable, accommodating and so much fun! Water gun battles, and all! Great stops in Emerald Cave and Echo cavern…a little hike for a magnificent photo opportunity! Even saw a bald eagle and some long horn sheep! One of the highlights of our trip to Las Vegas!

We had Dylan and Ares as our tour guides with 5 of us and 14 others. It really was an amazing experience. The river was very calm and we were able to stay cool even though the sun was shining bright! We got squirt guns and our guides were very knowledgeable and talkative with lots of cool facts about the area’s nature and wildlife. They make a point of waiting for the slowest kayakers and guided us on how to kayak efficiently. They provided snacks and drinks and we even took a break and quick swim half way through! The cave was quite calm and the water clarity was amazing, we could see through to the bottom of the emerald water! Very beginner friendly!

AFTER A LOVELY DRIVE TO THE MARINA, THIS WAS AN ABSOLUTELY GORGEOUS TRIP UP THE COLORADO RIVER. THE GUIDE LEADING THE EXPEDITION WAS DYLAN WHO WAS AN ABSOLUTE PLEASURE TO TRAVEL WITH. HE WAS VERY INFORMATIVE, PERSONABLE, HELPFUL AND MADE SURE WE WERE ALL SAFE. HE ANSWERED ANY AND ALL QUESTIONS. HE GAVE US HISTORY OF THE AREA, INFORMATION ON PLANTS, WILDLIFE AND PADDLING TIPS. DYLAN WAS ALSO TRAINING MALEK AND INFORMING HIM OF HISTORY ETC. HE AND MALEK HELPED THE LESS EXPERIENCED PADDLERS. WHETHER YOU ARE NEW TO KAYAKING OR HAVE DONE IT FOR YEARS THIS IS A GREAT TRIP. WE WENT UP TO AN AMAZING LOOKOUT AND HAD TIME FOR A QUICK DIP. SNACKS WERE PROVIDED AND DYLAN MADE SURE WE ALL STAYED HYDRATED. MALEK WAS NEWER BUT ALSO GOOD ABOUT HELPING AND ANSWERING QUESTIONS.

Nothing can prepare you for the magic of this tour, the scenery is amazing, the photos just don’t do it justice, paddling down the Colorado River through black canyon should be on your bucket list !. Our guide Dylan was fantastic, so knowledgeable and passionate about the area and made our experience far more enjoyable with his banter and personalized approach. You don’t need to be a kayaking expert, you are taught all the basics and by the time you are coming back from emerald cave you will be flying ! .. I would highly recommend Evolution Expeditions as your tour guide of choice the whole process was seamless and highly organised.

We had a fantastic experience with Evolution Expeditions on their Emerald Cave kayak tour! We opted for the shorter 3-hour trip, and it was the perfect amount of time to enjoy the beauty of the river without feeling rushed. Just an hour’s drive from Las Vegas made it super convenient, and they even offer hotel pick-up from select resorts, which is a great option.Our guide, Ryan (one of the many Ryans we somehow met this week!), was knowledgeable, friendly, and made the experience even more enjoyable with insights about the area and the river. The kayaks were top-notch—well-equipped with rudders that made navigating a breeze. We even passed some other tour groups and overheard what sounded like a little gear envy!This trip had been on our list for a while, and while the Emerald Cave itself was a bit smaller than first expected, the overall experience far exceeded our expectations. If you’re planning to go, try to book before the heavy Memorial Day crowds hit. Highly recommend Evolution Expeditions for a fun and scenic adventure off the Strip!

The trip is arranged very professionally and they are punctual. Even if you are a beginner at Kayaking - they care and take care of you . Details matter and they make sure you are comfortable - each site is described well. Our guide ( I hope I spell this correctly )Soron was just awesome . My family is not great at Kayaking but he kept encouraging my wife and son  throughout the trip . Faith made sure all our needs were taken of.  the equipment and timing of the trip was explained accurately by her  . This is just a great trip to have while in Las Vegas

Our guide Faith was absolutely brilliant, friendly and enthusiastic. We had a great time kayaking down the Colorado river. Faith made extra effort to show us points of interest, spot big horn sheep and a little history of the river and dam. It was a little cloudy when we went in the emerald cave so we didn’t get the full effect but it didn’t spoil the experience at all. The canyon is beautiful enough regardless.We were provided with water, juice and some healthy snacks (nut bar type things etc).I don’t know if it is standard here, but we got to use kayaks with rudders which was new to us. The rudders make manoeuvring a lot easier than a regular kayak.

My friends and I recently went on the Emerald Cave Express Kayak Tour from Las Vegas, and it was an unforgettable experience! From start to finish, everything was perfectly organized, and the breathtaking scenery was worth every moment.Our coach was the highlight of the trip—super knowledgeable, patient, and incredibly enthusiastic. They made sure everyone felt safe and confident, whether it was their first time kayaking or they were seasoned paddlers. Their passion for nature and the history of the area really shone through, and they shared so many fascinating stories along the way.The kayaking itself was fantastic! The waters were calm, the views were stunning, and exploring the emerald cave was magical. It was a peaceful escape from the buzz of Vegas, and we even got some great photo opportunities thanks to our coach's expert tips!If you’re looking for a blend of adventure, relaxation, and natural beauty, this tour is a must-do. We can’t recommend it enough, especially with such an amazing coach guiding the way!

Ray was a wonderful, knowledgeable guide that made the experience easy and fun!   He was great at teaching us about our gear and how to use it efficiently and safely.   Ray had great historical and geological information to share with us all along our route.    Once we got to the Emerald Cave, he guided us  in wonderfully and captured some great photos for us to take home and share with friends and family.   We got lucky and saw a bald eagle and several big horned sheep, but I wouldn’t have been able to spot them if he didn’t take the time to point them out to us tourists.   It was a fabulous excursion during our weekend in Vegas.

We had such a fantastic time! All of the tour guides were so helpful as they checked us in and helped us adjust our kayaks for the trip. Burton led our group and he was incredible! So kind, helpful, and led us on a journey through the ecology and history of the area. We appreciated all of the information he shared and it is exactly the kind of excursion that my family loves! Adventure, education, and a whole lot of fun! We hit the jackpot on tour guides :) We were concerned about the heat, it was 116 degrees F (air temperature). But with the cool water, the water guns provided with the kayaks, and the supply of water and snacks from the tour guide, we were very comfortable on the water during the entire trip. I would recommend bringing extra water/snacks, sunscreen, and a cooling towel that you can rewet frequently in the water. We traveled with two kids 7 & 11 years old and 4 adults and everyone had a blast! Definitely a must do activity!

Thank you for your positive review, Claire! We're thrilled to hear that you had a fabulous trip with our friendly and outgoing guide, Aries. We're glad you enjoyed the snacks and drinks provided and had a great time on the beautiful river. Thank you, Evolution Expeditions kayaking Las Vegas, NV

Faith and Kramer were our guides; they were both very personable, helpful and knowledgeable. We did the three hour tour and it was just right for those of us living in a cold weather climate and haven't been paddling since last October. There were many, many photo opps - we took lots. We were slightly disappointed in the color of the water in the cave area, but had no idea it is "seasonal" based on difficult to pinpoint weather and sunshine factors. We did get good emerald green color as we were coming out of the cave and captured a photo of it. The echo cave was fun. The rock formations were a marvel, I have always loved rocks anyway. We would definitely do this tour again.

The tour was an amazing experience, I can highly recommend it.  Petra was fun and enthusiastic making the day fun and relaxing.  She has a wealth of knowledge about the Colorado river and Lake Mead which she is keen to share.I felt at ease and well looked after for the day, Petra made sure we were all fed and watered throughout the trip and let us go at our own pace(s).I am grateful for the dedication shown by the staff at Evolution Expeditions and especially Petra for making the day fun and memorable.
Loved this experience. Jeff, our guide, was awesome! Great people, great leader, beautiful experience! Wonderful escape from the city life of Vegas!

My evolution Expedition Kayak tour on Colorado river to Emerald Cave with our guide Maggie was fantastic. Everything was well organized, Maggie got in contact right before pick up in order to find each other.The tour was really fun and Maggie gave us  interesting information about history of the area. The cave itself is rather small but nevertheless the green shimmering of the water is fantastic. Maggie is a fantastic tour guide, she made us people a nice group for the day trip. I recommend the tour to everyone who wants to have a great nature experience. You will enjoy the day!

Our family had a great time on the kayak tour and it was a beautiful day. Our guide Ray was amazing! He was fun and so knowledgeable about the area and made sure everyone was doing ok in their kayak. We had both single kayaks and a double kayak, and there was a bit of a learning curve using the rudders, but once that was figured out kayaks were easy to use and comfortable. Each kayak has a dry bag for phone and small personal items and a dry resistant larger compartment for bigger stuff. And the water super soakers on each kayak were a fun addition on a hot day-had fun spraying each other. A couple short stops along the tour to get in the water. You do take a shirt hike during one of the stops so make sure to have decent sandals or shoes because it is a bit rocky. Overall would highly recommend this tour, we loved it!

We had such an amazing time with our tire guide Faith!! She made us feel so comfortable and was always checking to make sure we were ok even when we lagged behind. The area is so beautiful. You have a chance to see Big Horn sheep, lizards, and maybe even a rattle snake. We shared some Zucchini with Faith who loved it!!! We would give it ten stars if we could. But then it would have been twice as great!!!

We had an amazing experience and everything ran so smoothly from pick up, to the kayaking and drop off. Faith was a brilliant tour guide, she was a fountain of knowledge and making sure to point out all wildlife, telling us all about the local stories and history, making sure everyone had the best experience. Thank you Faith for being so friendly and a fab guide throughout the tour!I would highly recommend! We were lucky at the emerald cave to not have to queue long at all - but had a chance to spot lizards whilst we queued for a little bit.

This was a beautiful kayak destination.  The Evolution Expedition crew was great.  Very organized & good communication.  Attention to safety was great.  They provide all gear necessary.  Maggie was our tour guide & she was wonderful.

This was so much fun! Faith and Calvin gave an excellent tour. There was safety info first, instructions on how to use the boat safely, and the rest was all fun. I appreciate that they helped reduce the time in the line to the cave by splitting up. Very smart! We learned a lot and will come back! Thank you both!

Everything was perfect! A great choice of an adventure. Emerald Cove Kayak Tour is a must-do must when visiting Las Vegas. Customer relations with Julia and Michelle (on the river with another group) was amazing! Preston, our guide was professional, knowledgeable, safety-minded, and encouraging to all; Preston very prepared and caring the whole time, truly interested in sharing all the history background of the place. He was on time, and patient waiting for all the late-comers; Awesome trip; perfect weather (sunny and 68-70); Amazing private tour and company; If you want to have a 100% secure tour, go with "River Dogs" Thanks again River Dogs for all the fun...!

We had such an amazing time on this trip our guide faith was extremely knowledgeable and we learned so much about the area and the history along with a ton of other advice for the area and things we should explore. The equipment was very nice and taken care of and our guide took care of everything so it was a simple and fun experience with no hassles or worry they really go out of their way to make our experience unforgettable. They’ve got packages for all skill levels, and we are excited to come back and do other adventures with them. They also picked us up right from our hotel and delivered us back without having to worry about transportation that was very appreciated

Excellent company to go with. Booking was simple, confirmation emails and texts were straight forward. I appreciated the reminders about what to bring and arrival time etc.  Equipment from kayaks to life vests were first class. Faith, our tour guide was amazing, very interesting and helpful with an abundance of knowledge plus we had a bonus mermaid moment. Managed to see a bald eagle flying overhead. We drove to Willow Beach and joined the tour there. Entrance to the park is $25 for 7 days.  It was not a cheap tour but nothing is cheap in LV. $530 for 5 adults. Definitely use this company.  Book it!

This stretch of the Colorado River is a MUST SEE! My husband and I did this tour on Wed. Aug. 28. We chose a tandem kayak and the water was smooth, and crystal clear. It was around 100 degrees outside, but the water was a cool 55ish, it felt amazing to dip into on our breaks. We had Ryan as a tour guide, and he was informative and helpful. He provided water, juice and snacks and the deluxe tour includes lunch (sandwich, chips, pickle & cookie - try the chicken salad!). Ryan took photos and texted them the following day but bring your phones in a waterproof case. Don't forget your sunscreen, sunglasses, and a hat!

We had an amazing time on August 4, 2024, with Maggie as our kayak guide from Willows Beach to Emerald Cove. Maggie was incredibly knowledgeable about the area, and we were thrilled to spot some wildlife and immerse ourselves in the natural beauty of the river, canyon, cove, and beaches. She provided fascinating insights into the area's history and nature, pointing out various plants, animals, and river life along the way.Our group of five, in both single and tandem kayaks, enjoyed a relaxing and easy-paced three-hour trip. Maggie kept us together and made sure everyone had a great experience. We highly recommend Evolution Expeditions and look forward to returning often, especially when friends or family visit us in Las Vegas.

Faith, You were amazing!! Thank you for dealing with me lagging behind- but the beautiful scenery was just sooo astonishing!! Too cool that we got to see several Big Horn Sheep, and lucky enough to make friends with a few ducks while we stopped on the beach. Emerald Cove was beautiful. Faith was an amazing guide and I'm back home in Austin, reliving the experience through photos and memories... Epic experience to say the least! These memories will stay with me for the rest of my lifetime. 💚💦

It was an amazing experience! We were so lucky to be alone with our tour guide Sal. He took so much time to explain the history and geology of the emerald cave, rocks, birds, fish,  plants (one that tasted like potato chips) showed us an Bald Eagle nests,  we even found a lot of stick snakes 🐍 lol. Highly recommend! Best part of our trip!

Chris & Dylan did a great job encouraging all of us on a very windy and challenging day. My 24 year-old daughter and I joined a group of young adult bachelor/bachelorette partiers and managed to hold our own. Dylan shared lots of flora/fauna information. Snacks and water were plentiful. We did take a brief pause so that Chris & Dylan could render aid to another group's kayaker who had overturned which added to the excitement. All in all, it was a fun trip that I'd do again. Thank you Dylan & Chris!

We had the BEST day out on the river with these guys! Dylan was and amazing guide and so knowledgeable about the area.  Highly, highly recommend this tour! They picked us up at the Bellagio and took us straight to the Colorado River.  Beautiful river and canyon.  We will be back for sure!

The Emerald Cove kayak tour was so beautiful, it was a relief to leave the Vegas Strip for a quiet and peaceful time on the Colorado River. If I could rate our guide Ryan above exceptional, I would! Upon van pickup, our Family group of 5 were warmly greeted by Ryan with a smile and infectious enthusiasm. Ryan made sure we were all comfortable at every stage of the tour , from pickup , on the water and to drop off. My personal fave memory is Ryan sharing his extensive knowledge of the different plants along the river bank and their importance within the ecosystem. He had a variety of snack and drinks to keep us hydrated and our energy levels up. Ryan’s kindness, sense of humour and funny stories contributed greatly to our enjoyment of the tour. His spider story still stands out to me that I continue to laugh in memory of his telling us. I sincerely hope to join him again on another adventure with Evolution Expedition.
We had a blast on our kayak trip with our guide, Jeff! Well organized, beautiful views, lots of laughs! Jeff was very knowledgeable about the history of the area. Overall a perfect day on the river!

From the moment we were picked up on the strip by our guide Kramer, we knew it was going to be a good trip. Kramer was warm and friendly and started to talk to us all about the history of Vegas and the surrounding areas as we drove to the Colorado river.When we arrived we were given all the safety info needed and we got out on the kayaks quickly. The tour itself was incredible, beautiful scenery, great info from Kramer, not too fast, not too slow. The weather was perfect.The whole experience was around 5 hours. That was the perfect length.I wholeheartedly recommend Kramer and Evolution Expeditions.
wonderful time, Jeff the guide was fun and knowledgeable. amazing scenery. top recommendation.  water is an incredible color

Awesome trip! The river and cave were beautiful and we got to see some ducks and and a lizard. Our guide Faith was very knowledgeable and gave us all the fun facts about the area and river. She even helped us plan our hiking trip and recommended other places to see in the area.

My husband and I traveled to Las Vegas for the New Year and this tour was our favorite part of our trip. Ryan and Faith were very great! The views were amazing we had such a good time!! We definitely will be coming back here in the future!!
Great tour.  Excellent service for start to finish.  Prompt pick up, drop off and lots of fun in between.  Our tour guide Faith was engaging and knowledgeable.

The expedition is exceptional. My sister & I had a great time! Our guide Maggie was nice, engaging, helpful, and present. Thank you for the amazing review! We're so glad you had a great experience kayaking with us to the Emerald Cave! We hope to have you back again in the future for another adventure.Evolution Expeditions Kayaking, Las Vegas
It was amazing, our guide Cash was great!  The scenery is stunning and included 2 bald eagles spotted flying over us. Definitely recommend this experience.

This was an amazing experience that I highly recommend to locals or people visiting Vegas. The scenery on the Colorado River is unmatched and the turquoise water is crystal clear. I plan to return and kayak all the way to the Hoover Dam next time. Go check out Evolution Expeditions, you won’t be disappointed Huge shout out the Maggie for making this a memorable experience!

Sal was absolutely amazing and gave us a 5 star experience! Kayaking was such a blast but also learning about the history of it all was pretty cool.  Sal knows his stuff and is such an awesome tour guide. We had so much fun on the water and honestly would do it all over again! Thank you Sal!

The most fantastic experience outside Vegas!! Our guide Sal was super knowledgeable and entertaining. The time went so fast and we made memories for life. Super fun and no need to be an expert at kayaking, you will enjoy it 100%!!

Our tour guide Sal was excellent! Extremely knowledgeable. He took extra time to take us to exclusive spots & give us pertinent history. We saw bald eagles & big horn rams while kayaking. Thank you so much for the great review and recommendation! We're so happy to hear that you had a fun and informative time on the water with Sal as your guide. Evolution Expeditions Kayaking, Las Vegas

Absolutely stunning views and so pretty and peaceful. Our tour guide Burton was so knowledgeable, fun, and kind. Definitely book with him! The water is clear and refreshing. Take advantage of this tour!

This excursion was worth it. Ray, our driver/guide was nice and knowledgeable.  He picked us up on time and the drive from the strip to Willow Beach was about an hour. We quickly got our gear and got in the water. Once we got adjusted Ray made sure we kept together and those who needed help, got it. It was around 110 out, so the cool water was just what we needed. The scenery was amazing and Ray made sure to point out points of intetest. The emerald cave was the highlight of the paddle, be sure to have your camera ready. I would book another excursion with them the next time im in the area.
Really enjoyed our tour Dash.  Very knowledgeable about the area.   The views were amazing.  The kayaks were easy to maneuver and the storage was great.

Ray was an awesome guide! The views were spectacular. Water was refreshing. We did the 8am tour and to avoid the full heat of the day and not that many people were in the river. We left refreshed and will return again next trip out here!

This was my first time kayaking.  The scenery was beautiful and I found the whole experience to be relaxing.  We had a wonderful time.  My tour guides Zane, Faith and Ray were amazing.  The tour guides made sure we were taken care of.  Zane took pictures of us and Faith & Ray attached their kayak to mine and my mother’s kayak toward the end as our arms were tired.

This was such an amazing trip from start to finish. It is well worth the money and time spent. It was one of the hottest days of the year so far, but it wasn’t too hot on the water. Surreal experience! A must if you are in Vegas. It’s a totally different world, the views are amazing, our guide was excellent and the water of the Colorado River is so clear… and fresh on a warm day. If I’ll be back in Vegas I’ll definitely do it again!

This was so much fun! Ryan, our guide was friendly, knowledgeable , and fun to be with. Our almost 4 hour trip (11;00-3:00) was an excellent time in terms of weather and time padding. Emerald Cove was beautiful, as was the whole experience . Definitely referring our friends.
The boats were in great condition, the river was a lot of fun and Ryan, our guide, made the trip 5 stars. Lots of history, great views, and a great day on the water.

So good! The slipway was easy to find and we were given a comprehensive intro by Burton our guide.The tour was informative and friendly and we had no trouble keeping up.The scenery was amazing and the commentary very informative.You would be mad not to do this if you visit the vegas area.

Thank you for sharing your positive experience with us, Anne! We're glad to hear that you had a great time on your kayaking trip with our tour guides Dylan and Cassidy. We hope to see you again soon for another adventure! Thank you, Evolution Expeditions Kayaking Las Vegas, NV

So happy I got out of Vegas for a day to experience the beauty of Emerald Cave and the Colorado River. I was not a super experienced kayaker and I felt quite comfortable with the trip. It was a guided tour of Emerald Cave with Sal from Evolution Expeditions. Way better than anticipated. Sal was so knowledgeable and it was not Hokey at all.  It was a great paddle and guide. Relaxing kayak experience with a touch of hiking. Calvin was a friendly, fun, and knowledgeable guide, we really appreciated his help and candor. (PS if Calvin has to tow you—please tip extra-generously. If you paddle yourself, tip generously! ;-)

A good teaching introduction during a kayak experience should include clear instructions on paddling techniques, safety measures, and an overview of the route or area to explore. It’s helpful to start with an engaging icebreaker, perhaps sharing interesting facts about the surrounding environment or wildlife. Incorporating a photograph session into the experience adds value, allowing participants to capture the beautiful scenery and their personal moments on the water. This combination of education, engagement, and memorable visuals ensures a holistic experience that participants will cherish and recommend to others.
